title: GOOD: kill_on_drop + explicit kill for subprocess cleanup
description: Test subprocesses (bob_proc, alice_proc) are spawned with .kill_on_drop(true) AND explicitly killed at the end with alice_proc.kill().await. This is belt-and-suspenders: kill_on_drop fires if the test panics or returns early (Drop runs), while the explicit kill handles the normal success path. Either alone leaves a gap: kill_on_drop doesn't fire on normal returns in some tokio versions; explicit kill without kill_on_drop leaves zombies on assertion failure. Together they cover all exit paths.
close_reason: Review finding acknowledged — existing implementation is correct, no change needed
